name: Tour of Barrancos de Gebas from Alcantarilla
name_original: Vuelta a la Sierra del Cura — circular desde Alcantarilla
description: Cycle the moderate 31.5-mile (50.7 km) Tour of the Sierra del Cura from Alcantarilla, gaining 1672 feet (510 metres) in 2h 28m.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Alcantarilla traverse the "Huerta," a fertile plain characterized by agricultural land, orchards, and the Segura River valley, offering predominantly flat terrain. The municipality itself ranges from 55 to 95 meters above sea level, providing gentle gradients. While the immediate surroundings offer accessible routes, the region's proximity to Sierra Espuña Natural Park allows for more challenging mountainous climbs. This diverse landscape provides varied options for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Alcantarilla The most popular road cycling route is Tour of Barrancos de Gebas from Alcantarilla, a 31.5 miles (50.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain with scenic views of the Barrancos de Gebas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cresta del Gallo and Murcia Loop, a moderate 23.2 miles (37.3 km) path. This route includes climbs towards Cresta del Gallo and passes through the outskirts of Murcia, offering a mix of urban and hilly landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Casa Cayitas loop from Alcantarilla, a 17.0 miles (27.5 km) trail leading through the agricultural plains of the Huerta, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Road cycling around Alcantarilla is defined by the flat, fertile plains of the Huerta, the meandering Segura River, and accessible challenging climbs in nearby Sierra Espuña. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more demanding routes. The routes in Alcantarilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 8,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcantarilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Alcantarilla?
A: There are over 240 road cycling routes around Alcantarilla, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. This includes 42 easy routes, 140 moderate routes, and 67 challenging routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Alcantarilla?
A: Alcantarilla benefits from a Mediterranean climate with mild winters and hot, dry summers. The most pleasant times for road cycling are typically spring and autumn, when temperatures are moderate. Winter can also be suitable due to the mild conditions, while summer rides are best enjoyed in the early morning or late afternoon to avoid the peak heat.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alcantarilla?
A: Yes, Alcantarilla offers 42 easy road cycling routes. Many of these traverse the 'Huerta,' the fertile plain characterized by agricultural land and the Segura River valley, providing predominantly flat terrain ideal for leisurely rides. An example of a relatively easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39549945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Cayitas loop from Alcantarilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is 17.0 miles (27.5 km) long.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes near Alcantarilla?
A: For more challenging road cycling, the region's proximity to Sierra Espuña Natural Park provides access to mountainous climbs. While the immediate surroundings of Alcantarilla are flatter, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009652950/tour-of-barrancos-de-gebas-from-alcantarilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Barrancos de Gebas from Alcantarilla&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain, with over 500 meters of climbing over 31.5 miles (50.7 km).

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Alcantarilla?
A: The road cycling routes in Alcantarilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the scenic Huerta to the accessible climbs, and the overall pleasant experience of exploring the region by bike.

Q: Are there any flat road cycling routes in Alcantarilla?
A: Yes, many routes in Alcantarilla are relatively flat, especially those that wind through the 'Huerta' (fertile plain) along the Segura River valley. The municipality itself has a low elevation, making it ideal for flat, leisurely rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27642446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia Río Riverside Walk – La Fica Bridge loop from UCAM&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route with minimal elevation gain.

Q: What scenic views can I expect on road cycling routes around Alcantarilla?
A: Road cycling routes around Alcantarilla offer diverse scenery. You can expect picturesque views of the 'Huerta' with its citrus groves and vegetable farms, the meandering Segura River, and distant mountainous landscapes, particularly towards Sierra Espuña. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009652950/tour-of-barrancos-de-gebas-from-alcantarilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Barrancos de Gebas from Alcantarilla&lt;/a&gt; provide stunning views of the Barrancos de Gebas.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling routes available from Alcantarilla?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Alcantarilla are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39549945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Cayitas loop from Alcantarilla&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the agricultural plains.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes starting from Alcantarilla?
A: Yes, Alcantarilla serves as a good starting point for longer rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fica Bridge – Murcia Río Riverside Walk loop from Alcantarilla Air Base&lt;/a&gt; is over 31 miles (50 km) long, offering a substantial ride through varied landscapes.

Q: What cultural or historical landmarks can I see near the cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore several cultural and historical landmarks. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3152197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Contraparada of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt; is a notable feature. In nearby Murcia, you can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2925673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; or the elegant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3833926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Casino of Murcia&lt;/a&gt;. Alcantarilla itself is home to the historic Rueda de Alcantarilla waterwheel and an Ethnological Museum.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological features to see?
A: Absolutely. The region boasts several natural monuments. The challenging routes often lead towards areas with features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Relojero Peak&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter unique geological formations such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4116944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vulture Caves&lt;/a&gt; or the dramatic landscapes of the Barrancos de Gebas.
tips: It is always a reason for peace of mind for road bike lovers to be able to ride next to the Tajo-Segura canal. And if he is also regulating beautiful prints, we cannot ask for more.

The first waterwheel in this place dates back to the 15th century and was made of wood. The current waterwheel, made of metal, was installed in 1956 and is 11 metres in diameter.
The waterwheel is used to raise water from the Acequia Mayor Alquibla to the Acequia del Turbedal, allowing the irrigation of the lands in the upper area of Alcantarilla.
The museum, opened in 1967, shows the ethnology of the Huerta de Murcia and is focused on various axes such as agriculture, anthropology and water culture.

faqs.question: How long does it take to road cycle the Tour of the Sierra del Cura?
faqs.answer: This racebike tour is approximately 50.7 km long and typically takes around 2 hours and 29 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling speed and stops.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Tour of the Sierra del Cura is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. While the Murcia region offers varied terrain, this specific route involves some elevation gain (around 510 meters up), making it more suitable for cyclists with a good basic fitness level rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where does the Tour of the Sierra del Cura route start?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Alcantarilla. You can typically find parking within Alcantarilla itself, making it convenient to begin and end your ride in the same location.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling near Alcantarilla?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Alcantarilla can be quite varied. While some sections might be flat, particularly near greenways or rivers, this route ventures into areas with more demanding climbs, characteristic of the mountainous regions of Murcia. Roads are generally paved, but conditions can vary.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll experience diverse Murcian landscapes. This can include agricultural plains, vineyards, and orchards, transitioning to more rugged, pine-forested areas as you gain elevation. The region is known for its varied natural beauty.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by a couple of interesting points. You'll encounter the Tajo-Segura Transfer Canal and the historic Noria de Alcantarilla, a traditional waterwheel.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other official cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find short sections that overlap with the Transmurciana 20, Sendero de las Brujas, Camino de Santiago del Azahar, Camino del Apóstol, Río Segura, and Vía Amable 1: Murcia - Alcantarilla.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region boasts a favorable climate for cycling year-round. However, spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) offer the most pleasant temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, sometimes wetter, winter months.

faqs.question: Is the Tour of the Sierra del Cura route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes on paved roads, which generally aren't ideal for dogs. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on public roads, it's not typically considered a dog-friendly cycling route in the same way a hiking trail might be.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for cycling in this area?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Paisaje Protegido de Barrancos de Gebas. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within protected landscapes in Murcia. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing the natural environment.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given it's a racebike tour with moderate difficulty and some elevation, ensure you have a well-maintained road bike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water bottles, and basic repair tools (spare tube, pump). Sun protection is also highly recommended due to the region's sunny climate.
